name: pydanticai-docs
description: Use this skill for requests related to Pydantic AI framework - building agents, tools, dependencies, structured outputs, and model integrations.
capabilities: Provide documentation for Pydantic AI agents and tools
Explain dependency injection patterns for AI applications
Show structured output examples using Pydantic models
Guide on using toolsets and model integrations
Offer best practices for production AI applications
author: DougTrajano
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance and Python code examples. No executable code, no file system access, no network calls, and no external commands are present. All static findings are false positives triggered by the documentation format and Python decorator syntax being misinterpreted as command execution patterns.
valueStatement: Building production-grade AI agents requires understanding type safety, dependency injection, and structured outputs. This skill provides comprehensive documentation and examples for the Pydantic AI Python framework.
userTitle: Build AI agents with Pydantic AI guidance
limitations: Does not execute code or install packages
Does not access local files or repositories
Does not make network requests to external APIs
Provides guidance only, no direct integration with user projects

useCases.title: Build type-safe AI agents
useCases.description: Create production-ready AI agents with proper type hints, dependency injection, and validated structured outputs.

useCases.title: Design agent architectures
useCases.description: Learn patterns for organizing tools, dependencies, and model integrations in scalable AI applications.

useCases.title: Implement structured outputs
useCases.description: Use Pydantic models to ensure LLM responses conform to specific schemas and data types.

promptTemplates.title: Basic agent setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating your first Pydantic AI agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a basic Pydantic AI agent with OpenAI and run a simple query.

promptTemplates.title: Add tools to agents
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I add custom tools to a Pydantic AI agent? Show me an example with @agent.tool decorator.

promptTemplates.title: Structured outputs
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use Pydantic models to get structured, type-safe output from my agent? Give me a complete example.

promptTemplates.title: Dependency injection
promptTemplates.scenario: Managing external services and API keys
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how to use dependency injection in Pydantic AI. How do I pass API keys and database connections to my tools?

bestPractices: Always define deps_type and output_type for better IDE support and type safety
Use dependency injection for database connections, API clients, and other shared services
Use Pydantic models for all structured outputs to ensure validated, type-safe responses
antiPatterns: Avoid using plain dictionaries instead of Pydantic models for outputs
Do not hardcode API keys in agent code - use dependency injection
Avoid creating agents without proper type definitions for dependencies and outputs

faq.question: Which LLM providers are supported by Pydantic AI?
faq.answer: Pydantic AI supports any model provider with tool-calling capability including OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Ollama.

faq.question: What Python version is required?
faq.answer: Pydantic AI requires Python 3.9 or higher. Check your Python version with python --version before installation.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Install via pip and import the Agent class.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: This skill only provides documentation guidance. No data is sent to external services or stored anywhere.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication in my agents?
faq.answer: Use the deps_type parameter to define a dependency class. Inject API keys through the deps argument when running agents.

faq.question: How is Pydantic AI different from LangChain?
faq.answer: Pydantic AI focuses on type safety and structured outputs using Pydantic models. It provides a simpler, more Pythonic API for building agents.
